issues.title: [Code improvement] Throw assert if calling API with block_ct_dim < full_ct_dim
issues.body:
We know that code paths that are triggered with block_ct_dim < full_ct_dim are not well tested and might have serious bugs.
To avoid silent failures due to using this code path, we should throw an assert to signalize that it shouldn't be used currently.

issues.title: [Blackhole] CFGSHIFTMASK instruction
Unpack 32x32 tiles
issues.body:
Currently the only unpacker kernel that unpacks 32x32 tiles is the llk_unpack_AB_matmul.h. The addition was done to saturate the compute kernel for matmul operations. There are other unpacker kernels that could also make use of the same additions:

```
    llk_unpack_A.h
    llk_unpack_AB.h
    llk_unpack_reduce.h
    llk_unpack_tilize.h
    llk_unpack_untilize.h
```

Not only would this be helpful for performance, but also it could remove the unnecessary face dimensions configs:

    config_unpacker_x_end<UNP_SEL>(face_r_dim);

That need to be added to init functions such as _llk_unpack_A_init_ to be able to fuse operations that switch between unpacking 16x16 and unpacking 32x32.

@nvelickovicTT fyi

issues.title: [Blackhole]: Pack untilize improvements
issues.body:
Pack untilize kernel needed to be re-written for Blackhole, due to Blackhole packer limitations:

Only one Dest offset register for the packer instance (but we can use strided mode of 16 rows apart)
PACR instructions can only write L1 contiguous output.
These L1 Offset registers, are now set per PACR Context, not per rows output from PACR instruction (i.e PACK_INTF_SEL)

```
THCON_SEC0_REG1_L1_Dest_addr_ADDR32 -> Context 0
THCON_SEC0_REG8_L1_Dest_addr_ADDR32 -> Context 1
THCON_SEC1_REG1_L1_Dest_addr_ADDR32 -> Context 2
THCON_SEC1_REG8_L1_Dest_addr_ADDR32 -> Context 3
```

Here is the current algorithm:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2368edba-a9ad-4e5c-9952-df51b1b474a6)

For wormhole b0, 8x16 rows can be output per tile (before incrementing counters), while for blackhole with the above implementation, only 2x16 can. Since pack_untilize feature needs to be fused with other operations, we cannot change the unpacker to use a different unpacking scheme (Fastest untilize scheme would be T0F0, T0F1, T1F0, T1F1, T0F2, T0F3, T1F2, T1,F3)). However, we should use that implementation if the use case is doing a pack_untilize for a block of tiles (i.e not fused).

One major optimization, is to enable the use of contexts.
```
THCON_SEC0_REG1_L1_Dest_addr_ADDR32 = CNTX0 = tile_offset (Configured for top faces)
THCON_SEC0_REG8_L1_Dest_addr_ADDR32 = CNTX2 = tile_offset + block_ct_dim * TILE_C_DIM *datum_size ((Configured for bottom faces)
```
Then algorithm can do 4x16 rows, will change to something like this:

```
CH0 x_stride = number of bytes per datum (2 bytes for Float16b default)
CH0 y_stride = FACE_C_DIM*x_stride
CH0 z_stride = FACE_R_DIM*y_stride (1x16x16 datums default, already done in pack_hw_config)
CH0 w_stride = 4*z_stride (32x32 by default)
PACK_INTF_SELECT_0 = 0b0011 (read 2 rows from dest)
PACK_INTF_SELECT_1 = 0b1100 (read 2 rows from dest)
Dest Mode = DST_STRIDED_MODE (Each row read from dest is 16 apart)

    for face_r_dim (16 by default):
        for block_ct_dim (2 in this example) {
            PACK(CNTX0, PACK_INTF_SELECT_0); (Reads 2 rows, writes them contiguous in L1, dest rows = 0,16, 1, 17, 2, 18 .....)
            PACK(CNTX1, PACK_INTF_SELECT_1); (Reads 2 rows, writes them contiguous in L1, dest rows = 32,48, 33, 49,  .....)
            W_CNT += 1 (Jumps to next tile)
        }
        Y_CNT+=1
    }
```
The problem here is if contexts are used, initial hardware configuration for data formats, tile sizes, etc, all need to also be programmed.

issues.title: Add asserts before semaphore posts and gets in LLK code.
issues.body:
Due to some race conditions for example one explained here 

https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/issues/19201#issuecomment-2867613877

we can have semaphore_get and semaphore_post instructions dropped, for example trying to call semaphore_get on a semaphore that is already 0. These could lead to very expensive  bugs. We need to add watcher asserts to make sure these situations do not arise. The timing due to enabling watcher may change and some bugs may not manifest, but still it may expose many bugs.

issues.title: Wrong registers set during configuration/initialization for packer.
issues.body:
Some registers or flags are adjacent, and to optimize a write to them, developers often used only one call to cfg_reg_rmw_tensix function trying to pack more data to do multiple tasks, but in blackhole some registers were not adjacent anymore. Thus the copied over code is invalid. The case I have encountered so far will not likely give wrong results but will consume more power. 

Moreover the helper structure to write into registers may not correspond to the original register ordering. 

File : ``` tt_llk_blackhole/common/inc/cpack_common.h```

Examples 

```
   inline void reconfigure_packer_l1_acc(const std::uint32_t pack_l1_acc){

      const uint32_t pack_l1_acc_disable_pack_zero_flag = pack_l1_acc ? (0b11) : (0b00);

      cfg_reg_rmw_tensix<THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_ADDR32, THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_SHAMT, THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_MASK | THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_MASK>(pack_l1_acc_disable_pack_zero_flag);

   }
```

The optimization for Wormhole can be valid since we have the bits adjacent in wormhole but not in blackhole

Wormhole fields :
```
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_ADDR32 59
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_SHAMT 21
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_MASK 0x200000
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_RMW \
    THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_ADDR32, THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_SHAMT, THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_MASK

#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_ADDR32 59
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_SHAMT 22
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_MASK 0x400000
```

Blackhole fields:
```
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_ADDR32 71
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_SHAMT 19
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_MASK 0x80000
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_RMW \
    THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_ADDR32, THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_SHAMT, THCON_SEC0_REG1_Pack_L1_Acc_MASK

---- REMOVED ---- 

#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_ADDR32 70
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_SHAMT 2
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_MASK 0x4
#define THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_RMW                                                \
    THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_ADDR32, THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_SHAMT, \
        THCON_SEC0_REG1_Disable_pack_zero_flags_MASK
```

For the ground truth the structures like this , in the same file cfg_defins.h files should be used as they are generated using scripts from rtls.
All such instances should be looked at and fixed.

issues.title: Fix ordering of fields in pack_config_t with the actual ordering of the registers.
issues.body:
The structurre of pack_config_t for Wormhole_b0. can be seen here 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6dafc871-81cd-44c9-899a-f4571440383c)


The ordering of the registers in cfg_defines.h for Wormhole_b0 can be seen here

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b2b7b397-29d8-44e7-ba48-ae3384c1d74d)

As can be seen what is between src_if_sel and l1_src_addr is different between the pack_config_t and the original register structure. 
So we need to make sure the structure is okay for both wormhole and blackhole and make sure all corresponding function calls are corrected.

issues.title: Standardize instruction type for maintainability and readability
issues.body:
There are examples in the LLK code where our instructions are declared like this:
`static constexpr uint unpack_srca_zerosrc    = TT_OP_UNPACR_NOP(SrcA, p_unpacr_nop::UNP_ZEROSRC);`

To improve portability, readability, and type safety we should introduce a new definition:
`#define instruction_t uint32_t`,
and use `instruction_t` consistently whenever declaring or storing an instruction. 

This guarantees 32-bit width, which is important as `uint` is not a standard type and may vary across compilers.

issues.title: Move SFPU code from tt-metal to tt-llk
issues.body:
# Move SFPU functions to tt-llk and add testing — $[Amount], [Difficulty]

**Project/Repo:** [tt-llk](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-llk)  and [tt-metal](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal)
**Category:** Feature
**Bounty Status:** Open

---

## 📝 Background
Some LLK implementation of SFPU OPs (for both WH and BH) lack testing coverage, but live in the tt-metal repo. We need to move these down to the tt-llk repo and add tests to prevent regressions.

## 🎯 What Success Looks Like
- Below are listed the files containing OPs which need to either be moved or overwritten in tt-llk
- "move implementation to tt-llk" means to create a file in the tt-llk repo and move the implementation there so that it can be tested
- "overwrite llk implementation with tt-metal" means to just replace the existing llk implementation with the one from tt-metal and test that
- All these OPs need tests in the tt-llk infra written for them as well

WH:
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_abs.h -> overwrite llk implementation with tt-metal implementation
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_add1.h -> move implementation to tt-llk
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_cast_fp32_to_fp16a.h -> overwrite llk implementation with tt-metal implementation
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_max.h -> overwrite tt-llk implementation with tt-metal implementation
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_min.h -> move implementation to tt-llk

BH:
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_abs.h -> overwrite llk implementation  with tt-metal
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_add1.h -> move implementation to tt-llk
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_cast_fp32_to_fp16a.h -> overwrite llk implementation with tt-metal BUT UPDATE ADDRMOD TO 7
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_max.h -> overwrite llk implementation with tt-metal implementation
- [ ] ckernel_sfpu_min.h -> move implementation to tt-llk

## 💡 Problem to Solve
Moving the implementations to tt-llk, adding tests to verify each OP, extending the test-infra to enable these OPs.

## 🧭 Guidance & Starting Points
- unary SFPU OP testing is done in `tt-metal/tt_metal/third_party/tt_llk/tests/python_tests/test_eltwise_unary_sfpu.py`
- contact @rdjogoTT for support

## 🔎 Possible Approaches
- this bounty is meant to get you familiar with how the test-infra works, so looking at other tests and golden implementations is a good place to start
- using torch or math library versions of the OPs as a golden is one possible approach

---

## 📚 Resources
- [Reference documentation for getting started in tt-llk](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-llk/blob/main/tests/README.md)

issues.title: Reduce Improvement
issues.body:
Based on this issue https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/issues/22870 we should adapt native reduce (`llk_math_reduce.h` and `llk_unpack_AB.h`) to implement a block reduce:
* column reduce - MATH: GM/GAPOOL in a loop over `block_rt_dim`, clear dvalid for SrcA at each iteration, at the end clear dvalid for SrcB. UNPACK: UNPACR a single face of scaler into SrcB, then UNPACR a whole tile into SrcA in a loop over `block_rt_dim`
* row reduce -  MATH: GM/GAPOOL in a loop over `block_ct_dim`, clear dvalid for SrcA at each iteration, at the end transpose the result by moving from DEST to SrcB and clear dvalid for SrcB. UNPACK: don't forget haloize mode, UNPACR a single face of scaler into SrcB, then UNPACR a whole tile into SrcA in a loop over `block_ct_dim`
* scalar reduce - combine the two above, care about location of intermediate result in DEST and what dim is larger (first do reduce across larger dim)

issues.title: [subissue-guardrails] Clean up redundant llk_math_eltwise_binary_init
issues.body: There are two versions of `llk_math_eltwise_binary_init`, w/ and w/o operands. Let's remove the one w/o operands since it just sets `num_faces` to default four. Change the usage of the Low Level API in the Compute API appropriately.

issues.title: [Ease-of-Use] Closing LLK Contract Gaps
issues.body:
This issue represents _Closing LLK Contract Gaps_ User Story within our _Ease of Use_ Epic. The list is not conclusive, and can be seen [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1oOsQCDgBL_1BTEIhMfMd5Q5WnXmtSyfZVRt08-_4yZ8/edit?gid=0#gid=0) (internal access only).

These issues need to be closed in order to make our LLK code compliant to the LLK Contract defined during Red Team F2F in Nov '25.

issues.title: [subissue-guardrails] Check Data Format Handling for Math
issues.body:
According to HW team, unpack0_dst_format is used as the FPU data format, while unpack1_dst_format is used as the SFPU format. 

We should do some experiments and changes to make sure this HW behavior is clear in our SW:

1. Take for example `_llk_math_hw_configure_`.
2. Check if we're ever passing different formats from tt-metal for `srca_data_format` / `srca_data_format`
3. This can be checked in jit `chlc_unpack_data_formats.hpp` artifacts in `~/.cache/tt-metal-cache`
4. If different, we should check also if tt-metal data format inference logic doesn't change these to one common data format.
5. In the end, if we're sure that the passed data formats are different, we should confirm the behavior of the HW based on the premise.

issues.title: [subissue-guardrails] Check disable_src_zero_flag condition
issues.body:
This commit:
https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-llk/commit/3e042d77c93910731336fffb50ecf3961916feea

Introduced a condition for disabling zero flags on WH that takes into account uint16 data, but it's unclear why this setting depends on that particular data format. 

We should explore and explain/adapt in the following files:
`tt-metal/tt_metal/third_party/tt_llk/tt_llk_wormhole_b0/common/inc/cunpack_common.h`
`tt-metal/tt_metal/third_party/tt_llk/tt_llk_blackhole/common/inc/cunpack_common.h`
`tt-metal/tt_metal/third_party/tt_llk/tt_llk_wormhole_b0/llk_lib/llk_unpack_A.h`
`tt-metal/tt_metal/third_party/tt_llk/tt_llk_blackhole/llk_lib/llk_unpack_A.h`

issues.title: Expose separate transpose settings in llk_unpack_AB
issues.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

`llk_unpack_AB` currently has a single transpose setting that controls both `transpose_faces` and `within_face_16x16_transpose`. For reduce-row we only need `within_face_16x16_transpose`, but not transpose_faces.

It would be useful to expose separate init settings for these two, similar to how this is handled in `llk_unpack_A`.

### Describe the solution you'd like

_No response_

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: Optimize SFPU based reductions.
issues.body:
### Optimize SFPU based reduce

The SFPU based reductions can be optimized by a somewhat non-trivial percentage.  Here are the steps. 

For column reduce, we could remove one transpose only. we would still be summing up the elements in a column but in a different order like instead of adding elements from row 0 with 1 , 2, 3, you probably would be adding 0, 4, 8, 12.

For Wormhole, some of the nops required after some SFPU instructions can be removed by reordering the order of operations. 

For columnwise reduction 

We transpose 
we are doing a reduction across the registers (0 to 3 and result in 3) and (4 to 7 and result in 4) 
We transpose
again we are doing a reduction across the registers (0 to 3 and result in 3) and (4 to 7 and result in 4)
We add register 0 with 4

But we could just do 

 a reduction across the registers (0 to 3 and result in 3) and (4 to 7 and result in 4) 
 add register 0 with 4 
 transpose 
 a reduction across the registers (0 to 3 and result in 3)

In the previous approach the addition of 0, and 4, the first lanes computation matters only, in this approach when we add 0, and 4 useful addition is being done across all the lanes, thus reducing the total operations.

issues.title: [subissue-guardrails] Make DataCopyType an Enum Class
issues.body:
During https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-llk/pull/976 fvranic had a comment:
```
[tt_llk_blackhole/llk_lib/llk_math_eltwise_unary_datacopy.h](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-llk/pull/976/files/f4e15b661bc2309a9b7d291d76b8e7ca0dbe60ca#diff-b4807c8ff56066f4c41a5594fb7f502f2e4fa717029607dbda62242560837b3d)

    // Workaround for HW bug (budabackend#1372): unpack_to_dest (A2D) with broadcast OR 32-bit data needs bit 11 disabled
    // When type == A2D, unpack_to_dest is implied
    if constexpr (type == A2D)
Member
@[fvranicTT](https://github.com/fvranicTT)
fvranicTT
[4 hours ago](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-llk/pull/976#discussion_r2716348440)
• 
How hard would it be to start using DataCopyType::A2D instead? I know it's an enum, not an enum class, and this is allowed, but it being an enum means we're leaking symbols to the global namespace. We could convert all of the enums to enum classes, since it will make our lives easier along the way. We already had ODR violation issues in the past.
```

issues.title: Investigate tensix_sync in _llk_math_reconfig_remap_
issues.body: Comment in this function indicates that the `tensix_sync()` is used to wait on all DEST accesses before modifying registers. Need to investigate whether this can be replaced with a stallwait with thorough testing - if the `tensix_sync` waits for DEST accesses from another thread such as UNPACK, then replacing with a stallwait would lead to less safe behaviour.
